summary: Matched `mnEvent_8024CE74` by restoring a `static inline` `mnEvent_CountUnlocked()` wrapper around the unlocked-event count loop. The semantic code stayed nearly identical, but MWCC codegen changed the zero-initialization pattern from two independent `li` instructions to the expected data-dependent register copy, taking the function from 99.42%/99.4% to 100% with no other functions affected.

key_files.role: Final merged change: adds `mnEvent_CountUnlocked()` and calls it from `mnEvent_8024CE74`.
Reviewed earlier attempted data/HSD_ASSERT change; not part of final changed-files list.
Reviewed earlier attempted static-literal extern declarations; not part of final changed-files list.

what_changed: `mnEvent_8024CE74` no longer contains the unlocked-count loop directly; it declares `s32 count`, checks `g_debugLevel > 2`, then assigns `count = mnEvent_CountUnlocked()`.
Added `static inline s32 mnEvent_CountUnlocked(void)` in `mnevent.c` with local `s32 i` and `s32 count = 0`, looping `i < 0x33` and incrementing when `gmMainLib_8015CEFC(i) != 0`.
Removed the previous direct-loop form that had `int count = 0`, `int i`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, a separate `count = 0`, and `if (gmMainLib_8015CEFC(i))`.
Kept the existing debug shortcut behavior: `g_debugLevel > 2` returns `0x2A` before counting.
Verification reported `python configure.py && ninja` green, one new match for `main/melee/mn/mnevent`, and `mnEvent_8024CE74` reaching 100%.
smart_moves: Used a source-level structure change, not a semantic rewrite, to steer MWCC into the target register-initialization pattern.
Restored the static inline wrapper removed in PR #2447 because the wrapper shape was the matching-critical detail.
Copied a known local pattern from nearby menu code: `mnInfo_CountUnlocked` / `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars` style helpers.
Kept the fix narrowly scoped to `src/melee/mn/mnevent.c`, with the final diff affecting only the target function/helper.
After review, the merged result avoided unrelated data-section and `HSD_ASSERT` override work.
decomp_lessons: For MWCC, semantically equivalent C can differ at the register-allocation/codegen level; wrapping a loop in a `static inline` can preserve the expected inlined local-variable relationship.
If a target uses a register copy such as `addi r30, r31, 0` instead of a second immediate zero load, try defining related locals together inside an inlined helper rather than directly in the caller.
Look for sibling functions in the same module before inventing a new matching tactic; here the relevant precedent was `mnInfo_CountUnlocked` / `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars`.
Do not mix a focused code match with premature data/sdata cleanup unless the PR is explicitly about that; reviewers preferred landing the code match first.
Static literals used for assertions do not necessarily belong in headers as `extern` declarations.
naming_conventions: `mnEvent_CountUnlocked` follows the module-prefix helper style: `mnEvent_` plus descriptive PascalCase action.
The address-named exported/known function remains `mnEvent_8024CE74`; the descriptive name is used only for the new static inline helper.
The helper name mirrors nearby `mn/` conventions cited in the PR body: `mnInfo_CountUnlocked` and `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars`.
The final code uses project-style fixed-width `s32` for the helper return and count/index locals rather than plain `int`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR body identifies the mismatch as MWCC lowering direct local initialization to two independent zero loads: `li r31, 0` for `i` and `li r30, 0` for `count`.
The expected build used `addi r30, r31, 0` for the second zero value, implying a data-dependent register copy.
Putting `s32 i; s32 count = 0;` together inside a `static inline` count helper and then inlining it back into the caller produced the expected codegen.
The boolean test was written explicitly as `gmMainLib_8015CEFC(i) != 0` in the restored helper.
The decomp.dev bot reported one new match: `mnEvent_8024CE74` from 99.42% to 100.00%, with matched code overall increasing by 0.01% / 416 bytes.

review_feedback.feedback: Recommended merging only the `mnEvent_8024CE74` match and avoiding data-section work: "A human can easily match up the data sections for a TU once the code is matched."
Rejected an attempted local `HSD_ASSERT` override around `jobj.h` as premature sdata work: "This is slop. At best, it prematurely fixes sdata."
Rejected declaring `mnNameNew_804D4F84` / `mnNameNew_804D4F8C` in a header: "These are static literals and don't need to be declared anywhere."

follow_up_queries: Search for other `mn/` count loops where MWCC emits two independent `li` zero initializations but the target expects a register copy.
Audit functions affected by PR #2447 for removed static inline wrappers that may have carried matching-sensitive codegen.
Compare `mnEvent_CountUnlocked`, `mnInfo_CountUnlocked`, and `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars` for reusable menu count-helper structure.
Look for PRs where reviewers rejected `HSD_ASSERT` overrides or static-literal extern declarations as premature sdata matching.
